channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to over 1,000 as more bodies recovered from the rubble
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from a powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake in Myanmar jumped to over 1,000 on Saturday as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed when it struck near the country's second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: They fled Syria years ago. Now, they spend their first Ramadan back amid nostalgia, relief and loss
item.description: As they spend their first Ramadan in their country in years, many Syrians who’ve recently returned from abroad to a homeland they were forced to flee have been celebrating the end of the Assad family’s rule.
item.dc:creator: Mariam Fam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, destroying buildings, a bridge and a dam. At least 144 people were killed in Myanmar, the head of the military government said in the televised speech.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: United Kingdom could be only G7 nation not to produce its own steel; Chinese owner blames Trump tariffs
item.description: A Chinese steel group that owns British Steel is blaming U.S. tariffs for the potential closure of two blast furnaces that could impact the steel industry in Britain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli settlers seen on camera assaulting a Palestinian village. Police arrest only Palestinians
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Over a dozen Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian village in the southern Israeli-occupied West Bank on Friday, beating residents with sticks and rocks, in an incident captured with rare clarity by security cameras.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on Lebanon's capital for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Fears of civil war in South Sudan stem from political rivalry between the president and his deputy
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Tensions are rising in the oil-rich east African nation of South Sudan after Riek Machar, who serves as one of the country’s vice presidents, was arrested in the capital.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing nearly 700 people
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving nearly 700 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World Central Kitchen says Israeli strike killed Gaza volunteer
item.description: The World Central Kitchen (WCK) said that an Israeli military strike killed one of the charity group’s volunteers working in the Gaza Strip and injured six other people. “Our hearts are heavy today as we mourn the loss of one of our volunteers in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland lawmakers form a new government on the eve of Vance visit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Greenlandic lawmakers on Thursday agreed to form a new government, banding together to resist U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to annex the Arctic island, local media reported.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at a university in Turkey's capital
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Police used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey’s capital early Thursday, the latest clash in the country’s biggest anti-government protests in over a decade.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French president says not all European allies agree on a proposed force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France and Britain will continue to forge ahead with plans to deploy troops in Ukraine to defend an eventual peace deal with Russia but only some other nations want to take part, French President Emmanuel Macron said Thursday after a summit of countries that have been mulling the propos
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SAMUEL PETREQUIN and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Sudan opposition says peace deal has collapsed after arrest of its leader
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said Thursday a peace agreement that ended a five-year civil war has collapsed, following the arrest of its leader Riek Machar a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe says no to Trump push to lift sanctions on Russia, upending Black Sea ceasefire
item.description: Europe says no to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure some sanction relief for Russia as he tries to make a Black Sea ceasefire deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s centuries-old Gounsa temple is left in ruins by unprecedented wildfires
item.description: Wildfires in South Korea's southern regions have left 24 people dead and destroyed more than 300 structures, including large parts of an ancient Buddhist temple complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
